sentences.en: Today was Career Day at school, where different parents come in and talk about their jobs for ten minutes each.
sentences.zh: 今天是學校的職業日，會有不同的家長來，每人花十分鐘介紹自己的工作。

sentences.en: The first parent was an engineer who builds bridges, and he showed us photos of one bridge that took three whole years to finish.
sentences.zh: 第一位家長是負責建橋的工程師，他給我們看一座蓋了整整三年才完工的橋的照片。

sentences.en: The second parent was a lawyer, and she explained that her job involves reading a lot of long, boring documents so other people do not have to.
sentences.zh: 第二位家長是律師，她說她的工作就是讀一大堆又長又無聊的文件，這樣其他人才不用自己讀。

sentences.en: A police officer came next and told us a story about helping a lost child find his family at a busy shopping mall.
sentences.zh: 接著是一位警官，他跟我們說了一個在擁擠的購物中心幫一個走失小孩找到家人的故事。

sentences.en: Bernice's mother is a secretary at a big company, and she showed us how many phone calls she answers in just one single hour.
sentences.zh: Bernice 的媽媽是一間大公司的秘書，她讓我們看她一小時內要接多少通電話。

sentences.en: One parent works as a newspaper reporter, and she said the hardest part of her job is writing a whole article in less than an hour.
sentences.zh: 有一位家長是報社記者，她說她工作最難的部分就是要在不到一小時內寫完一整篇報導。

sentences.en: A famous local writer also came, and he told us he throws away almost as many pages as he actually keeps.
sentences.zh: 一位當地小有名氣的作家也來了，他跟我們說他丟掉的稿紙幾乎跟他留下的一樣多。

sentences.en: There was also a singer who performs at small restaurants on weekends, and she sang one short song for the whole class.
sentences.zh: 還有一位在餐廳週末駐唱的歌手，她為全班唱了一小段歌。

sentences.en: One student's uncle works as an actor, mostly in small roles, and he showed us a funny clip of himself falling down stairs for a scene.
sentences.zh: 有個同學的叔叔是演員，大多演小角色，他放了一段自己在戲裡摔下樓梯的爆笑片段給我們看。

sentences.en: A store clerk explained how to be polite to customers even when they are in a very bad mood, which sounded harder than it looks.
sentences.zh: 一位店員解釋要怎麼在顧客心情很差的時候還保持禮貌，聽起來比看起來難多了。

sentences.en: William's own school had career day too, and one parent visiting was a factory boss who runs a whole business making toys.
sentences.zh: William 的學校也有職業日，其中一位到場的家長是工廠老闆，經營一整個做玩具的事業。

sentences.en: William came home extremely excited, claiming the toy factory boss promised to send him a sample robot someday, which nobody in our family fully believes.
sentences.zh: William 回家超興奮，說那位玩具工廠老闆答應以後要送他一個機器人樣品，我們家沒有人完全相信這件事。

sentences.en: Sophia said she used to be a full-time housewife when Mom and my uncle were small, managing the whole house every single day.
sentences.zh: Sophia 說在媽媽跟我叔叔還小的時候，她曾經是全職家庭主婦，每天打理整個家。

sentences.en: She said that job never officially ends, since a house always needs someone paying attention to it.
sentences.zh: 她說這份工作從來沒有正式結束過，因為一個家永遠需要有人在照顧它。

sentences.en: By the end of the day, our teacher asked everyone to write down which job sounded the most interesting to us personally.
sentences.zh: 一天結束時，老師要每個人寫下自己覺得哪份工作聽起來最有趣。

sentences.en: I wrote down writer, since the idea of throwing away bad pages until you find good ones sounded exactly like keeping a diary.
sentences.zh: 我寫的是作家，因為那種一直丟掉不好的稿紙直到找到好的的過程，聽起來就跟寫日記一模一樣。

quiz.question: What does the engineer who visits Career Day build?
What does the lawyer say her job mostly involves?
options: Reading a lot of long, boring documents.

quiz.question: What job does Coco write down as the most interesting to her?
